critic_raw: The test set design uses 'one entire institution as the held-out test set' plus temporal future cases from training institutions. This creates a test set with two fundamentally different distribution shifts simultaneously: institutional shift AND temporal shift. If the multimodal model performs poorly on the test set, it is impossible to distinguish whether the poor performance is due to institutional generalization failure or temporal generalization failure. These should be evaluated separately.
defender_raw: Round 1: The design specifically addresses this: 'performance can be separately evaluated by institution and time period to detect domain drift or site-specific confounds.' The test set contains both the held-out institution AND temporal future cases from training institutions — these can be stratified by institution to separately evaluate institutional vs. temporal generalization. If the design evaluates both the held-out institution test set and the temporal future test set separately, the contributions of each source of distribution shift can be disentangled. The design explicitly builds in per-institution and per-time-period analysis.

rounds.critic_followup: The design says 'the final 20% temporal split + held-out institution' as the test set — this appears to combine both into a single test set rather than evaluating them separately. Does the design actually evaluate them as two distinct test sets?
rounds.defender_followup: The test set construction uses both the temporal future cases AND the held-out institution cases, but per-site and per-time-period macro-F1 are secondary metrics explicitly listed. This means the single test set is stratified in analysis: held-out institution cases are evaluated separately from temporal future cases from the known institutions. The design correctly separates the two types of evaluation through stratified analysis, not by creating two separate test sets. This is a valid approach — a unified test set with stratified analysis is equivalent to two separate test sets with combined reporting.

adjudication_raw: One concern: combined institutional and temporal shifts in test set prevent distinguishing failure types. Resolved: per-site and per-time-period macro-F1 stratification enables separate evaluation within the combined test set. Verdict: defense_wins.
